body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 25/2026
Made under: Provincial Sales Tax Act
Published in: No. 4 – B.C. Regs. 18 to 28, March 10, 2026
Deposit date: March 9, 2026

B.C. Reg. 25/2026, deposited March 9, 2026, under thePROVINCIAL SALES TAX ACT[section 240]. Order in Council 68/2026, approved and ordered March 9, 2026. On the recommendation of the undersigned, the Administrator, by and with the advice and consent of the Executive Council, orders that, effective May 1, 2026, the Designated Accommodation Area Tax Regulation, B.C. Reg. 93/2013, is amended as set out in the attached Appendix. — B. BAILEY,Minister of Finance; M. FARNWORTH,Presiding Member of the Executive Council. 1	Schedule 1 of the Designated Accommodation Area Tax Regulation, B.C. Reg. 93/2013, is amended in column 4 of item 23.1 by striking out "May 1, 2026" and substituting "May 1, 2031". (a)	in column 3 of item 1 by striking out "2%" and substituting "3%", and (b)	in column 4 of item 1 by striking out "August 1, 2026" and substituting "May 1, 2031". Index Page|Previous Regulation|Next Regulation|Cumulative Index Page Copyright © 2026: King's Printer, Victoria, British Columbia, Canada

minutes_briefs.title: Accommodation area tax rate rises to 3% in BC, extended to 2031
minutes_briefs.summary: The BC government has amended the Designated Accommodation Area Tax Regulation to increase the tax rate from 2% to 3% in certain designated areas, and extended the tax's expiry date from May 1, 2026 to May 1, 2031. This means businesses in those areas must collect and remit a higher tax on accommodation starting May 1, 2026.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Update your billing system to collect 3% (instead of 2%) on accommodation in designated areas, effective May 1, 2026.

key_dates.label: Accommodation area tax rate increase to 3% takes effect
Accommodation area tax expiry extended to May 1, 2031
